claim: Sixth MCC measurement, first with the per-rule event-count detector (Session 7). Balanced n=40 sweep at scale=0.05, t_end=0.5s, Rust + 4-worker parallel. Result: MCC=0.125 (TP=5, FP=3, TN=17, FN=15). LOWER than v4 (0.229), not higher. The per-rule detector catches more metabolic essentials than the ShortWindowDetector (5 vs 1) but also picks up 3 false positives on Breuer-nonessential catalytic genes (0034, 0228/lpdA, 0732/deoC), and misses all non-catalytic essentials (15 FN, mostly ribosomal / tRNA synthetase / replication). Side-result: on the 4-gene reference panel (pgi, ptsG, ftsZ, 0305) the detector hits MCC=0.577 - close to the brief's 0.59 - but that n=4 number is sample-size noise.

reference_panel_side_result.note: pgi + ptsG correctly caught via catalysis_silenced; ftsZ correctly returns no_catalytic_rules; 0305 (Quasiessential) has no catalytic rules so is missed. Close to brief target but n=4 is not a meaningful measurement.

gene_to_rules_map_summary.note: 114 / 458 CDS have at least one catalysis:* rule (the 'metabolic enzymes' subset).

false_positives.evidence: silenced CHOLt, ADEt_syn, etc (12 transport rules)
false_positives.interpretation: transport system with biological redundancy not in the simulator model

source_detail: Predictions: outputs/predictions_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1_w4_cal10sf2.0_per-rule.csv. Metrics: outputs/metrics_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1_w4_cal10sf2.0_per-rule.json. Reproducible via: python scripts/run_sweep_parallel.py --max-genes 40 --balanced --calibrate 10 --workers 4 --use-rust --scale 0.05 --t-end-s 0.5 --detector per-rule
caveats: MCC=0.125 is below the predicted 0.35-0.45 range from NEXT_SESSION. Reporting the honest number rather than inventing post-hoc explanations to inflate it.

The 3 false positives are biologically real signals from the simulator but biologically wrong against Breuer: the simulator treats each catalysis rule as essential for its reaction's products, but Breuer's labels account for pathway redundancy (alternate enzymes, salvage pathways) that the simulator does not model.

15 of 20 essentials in the balanced panel are missed because they are non-catalytic (ribosomal proteins 0199/0257/0637/0658/0025/0372/0518/0775, translation factors 0669/0528, etc). The PerRuleDetector is architecturally incapable of catching these - they do not appear in catalysis:* rules at all. This is expected behaviour per the detector's design doc.

Reconciling the per-rule false-positive mismatch with Breuer requires either: (a) modelling pathway redundancy in the simulator (alternate enzymes, backup pathways), or (b) a rule-scoring function that weights rules by their unique necessity (e.g. 'only gene catalysing this reaction' vs 'one of N enzymes'), or (c) composing the per-rule detector with a secondary signal that requires downstream-pool-disruption confirmation.

Non-catalytic essentials (ribosomal, tRNA synthetase, replication) remain architecturally uncatchable by ANY short-window detector. The fix is Path A from REPORT.md: longer bio-time runs where ribosome/translation pool collapse becomes visible. That's a compute commitment outside the current interactive session budget.
Side-result: on the 4-gene reference panel the detector hits MCC=0.577, near the 0.59 target. That is optimistic sampling; the balanced n=40 number is the honest one.
